input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade, the .testsettings file seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

data.content:

data.content:
In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are **no longer supported**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's how to migrate each setting from your `local.testsettings`:

**Create a `.runsettings` file:**

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Test timeout (was Properties/TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution / deployment (was Execution/TestTypeSpecific in .testsettings) -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector (was AgentRule/DataCollectors in .testsettings) -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution/test deployment config
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The configuration needs to be migrated to a `.runsettings` file and/or MSTest attributes. Let me provide guidance on how to migrate each setting.

    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector (was AgentRule/DataCollectors in .testsettings) -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"

                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
**Summary of the migration:**

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution><TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" />` | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled></MSTest>` (SDK-style projects don't need deployment) |
| `<DataCollector uri="...CodeCoverage/1.0">` | `<DataCollectors>` section under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

**To use the new file**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add to your `.csproj`:
  ```xml
  <PropertyGroup>
    <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  ```

You can now safely delete `local.testsettings`.
